results.name: csv_to_json
results.prompt: convert orders.csv to JSON in orders.json
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e orders.json ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: orders.json" >&2
  exit 1
fi
/home/uphill/programming/py/oss-testing/.venv/bin/python -m nlsh.csv_to_json orders.csv > orders.json

results.name: json_filter
results.prompt: from users.json keep rows where role is not guest into member_users.json
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e member_users.json ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: member_users.json" >&2
  exit 1
fi
jq --arg field role --argjson value '"guest"' 'map(select(.[$field] != $value))' users.json > member_users.json

results.name: json_select_fields
results.prompt: from tickets.json keep only ticket_id priority and owner into ticket_queue.json
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e ticket_queue.json ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: ticket_queue.json" >&2
  exit 1
fi
jq --argjson fields '["ticket_id", "priority", "owner"]' 'map(. as $row | reduce $fields[] as $field ({}; . + {($field): $row[$field]}))' tickets.json > ticket_queue.json

results.name: json_sort
results.prompt: sort invoices.json by total descending into invoices_sorted.json
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e invoices_sorted.json ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: invoices_sorted.json" >&2
  exit 1
fi
jq --arg field total 'sort_by(.[$field]) | reverse' invoices.json > invoices_sorted.json

results.name: json_group_count
results.prompt: count incidents.json rows per severity into incidents_by_severity.json
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e incidents_by_severity.json ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: incidents_by_severity.json" >&2
  exit 1
fi
jq --argjson fields '["severity"]' --arg count_field count 'group_by([.[$fields[]]]) | map(.[0] as $first | (reduce $fields[] as $field ({}; . + {($field): $first[$field]})) + {($count_field): length})' incidents.json > incidents_by_severity.json

results.name: pdf_merge
results.prompt: merge contract_a.pdf and contract_b.pdf into contract_packet.pdf
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e contract_packet.pdf ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: contract_packet.pdf" >&2
  exit 1
fi
qpdf --empty --pages contract_a.pdf contract_b.pdf -- contract_packet.pdf

results.name: pdf_extract_pages
results.prompt: extract pages 2 through 3 from handbook.pdf into handbook_excerpt.pdf
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
if [ -e handbook_excerpt.pdf ] && [ "${NLSH_ALLOW_OVERWRITE:-0}" != "1" ]; then
  echo "Refusing to overwrite existing file: handbook_excerpt.pdf" >&2
  exit 1
fi
qpdf handbook.pdf --pages . 2-3 -- handbook_excerpt.pdf

results.name: find_files
results.prompt: find every PDF under ./contracts with executed in the path
results.compiled_script:
set -euo pipefail
mapfile -d '' -t MATCHES < <(find ./contracts -maxdepth 2 -type f -path './contracts/*executed*.pdf' -print0 | sort -z)
if [ "${#MATCHES[@]}" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "No matching files found." >&2
  exit 1
fi
printf "%s\n" "${MATCHES[@]}"
